purpose: Formal contract for every write to any SSOT file. Every change is one transaction: identified, hashed before/after, receipt-linked, attributable, and reversible by rule. Source of truth for the rule referenced by architecture_plan_fact_gap.json ssot_update_protocol.

transaction_required_fields.state_change_id: uuid4, generated per transaction, never reused
transaction_required_fields.trace_id: links to the task/dispatch event that caused this change
transaction_required_fields.target_ssot_file: exact repo path of the file being changed
transaction_required_fields.pre_state_hash: sha256 of target file content immediately before change
transaction_required_fields.post_state_hash: sha256 of target file content immediately after change
transaction_required_fields.receipt_ref: path to governance/proofs/ file justifying this change; MUST exist before transaction is considered valid
transaction_required_fields.applied_by: role, not persona — Curator-Executor | Auditor | External Auditor | Operator

validity_rule: A change to any file listed in ssot_canonical_files (see ssot_precedence_order.json) without a corresponding entry in governance/state/ssot_transaction_log.jsonl bearing all 9 fields above is INVALID.
hard_norm_per_bem010_audit_item_4: This is not merely 'detected by the next auditor pass.' Any SSOT-file write lacking a matching ssot_transaction_log.jsonl entry is, by definition and without exception, an automatic protocol violation that downgrades the affected area's release_status to STATE_INCONSISTENT immediately upon detection — not as a discretionary auditor judgment, but as a mechanical rule identical in force to the STATE_INCONSISTENT trigger in ssot_conflict_handling_protocol.json. The untransacted write itself is the violation; no separate finding is required to trigger the downgrade.
known_bootstrap_exception: All SSOT writes made during BEM-008 through BEM-011 sessions (prior to this protocol's own creation and TXN-1 enforcement implementation) are retroactively grandfathered as pre-enforcement and are NOT flagged STATE_INCONSISTENT. This exception applies only to writes with timestamps before TXN-1 enforcement guard is implemented and logged as such in ssot_transaction_log.jsonl. Any write after that point without a transaction entry is a full violation with no exception.
